arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta issues PGMOL rallying cry as Arsenal overcome history at Manchester City
arts.sum: After two anaemic snore-fests last season, Arsenal and Manchester City served up an all-timer of a first half. Erling Haaland was allowed far too cheap a goal, and when Ilkay Gundogan struck the post with a free kick, it really felt like a matter of when not if City would find a second.

arts.ttl: Why Man City have been forced to play again just 48 hours after gruelling Arsenal match
arts.sum: Manchester City have only just caught their breath from this weekend's clash with Arsenal but will have to lace up again with just a short rest thanks to their rivals

arts.ttl: Arsenal make Trossard decision as transfer claim emerges ahead of January
arts.sum: Speaking after that decision, Arteta said: "I was amazed. football.london says: Despite struggling to tie down a regular spot in Arteta's team and often playing as second fiddle to Gabriel Martinelli, Trossard has done a solid job when called upon.

arts.ttl: Arsenal news: Mikel Arteta receives brutal title blast as star adds to injury woes
arts.sum: Fall out from Arsenal's draw away to Man City continues with Mikel Arteta set to address the media today following criticism from a number of Pep Guardiola's players

arts.ttl: Richard Keys claims Michael Oliver 'dropped a b******' by not cautioning Jeremy Doku before sending Leandro Trossard off in Arsenal's hot-tempered draw with Man City
arts.sum: The Spaniard was left incensed by Leandro Trossard 's two yellow cards and clearly made his feelings known on the booking for delaying a restart.

arts.ttl: Arsenal defender Gabriel warns Man City his side are 'waiting for them' at the Emirates Stadium as he breaks his silence on Erling Haaland's 'provocative' ball throw at the Etihad
arts.sum: Arsenal centre-back Gabriel Magalhaes has issued a warning to title rivals Manchester City after Sunday's fierce encounter at the Etihad.

arts.ttl: Papers: PL clubs complain to PGMOL over Arsenal's use of 'dark arts'
arts.sum: The top stories and transfer rumours from Tuesday's newspapers...

arts.ttl: Rodri spotted heading to hospital consultation in Barcelona as Man City fear key midfielder has suffered season-ending injury against Arsenal
arts.sum: Manchester City midfielder Rodri has been spotted attending a specialist consultation at a hospital in Barcelona as the Premier League champions look for a diagnosis on his injury.

arts.ttl: Arsenal could play 16-year-old goalkeeper Jack Porter against Bolton with David Raya likely to be rested after picking up a knock at Man City and Pedro Neto cup tied
arts.sum: Arsenal could be forced to play 16-year-old rookie goalkeeper Jack Porter after David Raya limped off in Sunday's enthralling 2-2 draw against Manchester City.

arts.ttl: New footage obtained by Mail Sport appears to show Arsenal youngster Myles Lewis-Skelly sent to communicate with David Raya before keeper went down in time-wasting storm
arts.sum: EXCLUSIVE BY JACK GAUGHAN: Michael Oliver observed the 17-year-old communicating with Raya before the Arsenal goalkeeper fell to the floor and stopped proceedings.
